_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: GA federal House batch-6: eugene-yu (GA-01 primary loss status fix + cited positions), jesse-petrea + james-burchett (mis-filed as GA-01 federal candidates — neither was on the GA-01 ballot; converted to their actual sitting GA House seats HD-166/HD-176 and evidence-scored at state tier), sanford-bishop (D GA-02 incumbent, full federal evidence), brian-jack (R GA-03 freshman incumbent, full federal evidence). All claims cited; nulls where no evidence.

set.office: U.S. Representative GA-01 (2026 R Candidate · LOST primary 2026-05-19)

profile.birthplace: South Korea (immigrated to Georgia 1974, naturalized citizen)
profile.education: Butler HS Augusta (1974); criminal justice, Augusta College; Harvard Business School (1996, per iVoterGuide)
profile.background: Korean-American businessman; Augusta firefighter (1975–77), U.S. Army MP (1977–80), Richmond Co. sheriff's deputy (1980–85); former National President, Federation of Korean American Associations USA; perennial candidate (US Senate 2014 withdrew; House GA-12 2014/16/18, GA-07 2020, GA-06 2022, GA-04 2024 lost to Hank Johnson)
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — LOST the 2026-05-19 GA-01 R primary: 3,083 votes (4.4%), 5th of 6. Jim Kingston won outright 52.4% (no runoff). Positions scored from his own cited platform/survey statements (social-media protocol).

eugene-yu.notes_set: LOST 2026-05-19 GA-01 R primary with 4.4% (5th of 6); Jim Kingston won outright 52.4%, no runoff (Wikipedia results table; WTOC; NBC). Positions from his own platform + iVoterGuide survey. FLAGS: (1) abortion contradiction — 2016 iVoterGuide survey accepted life/rape/incest/fetal-anomaly exceptions, but 2026 site claims '100% pro-life. No exceptions'; (2) marked NEUTRAL on one-man-one-woman marriage definition on iVoterGuide (deviation), though supports FADA conscience protections; (3) iVoterGuide panel rated him 'Moderate (40)'; (4) interventionist lean — strongly disagreed with waiting for direct threats before military action. Hyundai-plant statement distinguished lawful-visa Korean workers from illegal aliens (supports deporting criminal aliens).

0.note: Signed survey: 'Human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection at every stage until natural death'; 2026 site: '100% pro-life.'

1.note: No abolition advocacy documented; 2016 survey accepted exceptions (contradicts 2026 'no exceptions' slogan — see notes).

3.note: Survey: legal protection 'at every stage until natural death.'

0.note: Marked NEUTRAL on whether government should define marriage exclusively as one man and one woman — declined to affirm (documented deviation).

1.note: Supports the First Amendment Defense Act protecting religious objectors; agrees religious liberty is at risk.

0.note: Platform: CBDCs 'may present the unacceptable threat of creating a cashless society'; supports crypto innovation.

1.note: Names 'a sound-money monetary system' as a priority but no gold/silver or Fed-audit position documented.

0.note: Platform: hand-marked, hand-counted paper ballots; opposes Dominion voting machines.

1.note: Survey: disagrees people should vote without photo ID.

0.note: Survey 'Strongly Agree' on border wall and infrastructure for complete control of entry/exit; reaffirmed 2026.

1.note: Supports deporting criminal aliens (Trump policy); no position on deporting ALL illegal aliens incl. minors. Hyundai statement defended lawful-visa workers.
Survey: 'Strongly Disagree' that more restrictive gun laws are needed; rejected gun-blame after Kirk assassination.

set.office: State Representative (GA House District 166 · Savannah/Chatham · sitting · 2026 re-election)

profile.education: Benedictine Military School; B.S. Biology, Armstrong State College (1989)
profile.background: Healthcare businessman — 28 yrs co-owner Altrus Assisted Living + Coastal Home Care (sold 2019); GA House since Jan 2015; Chair, Human Relations & Aging Committee; Appropriations, Health, Public Safety committees
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — RECORD CORRECTED: prior record mis-filed him as a 2026 GA-01 federal candidate; he was NEVER on the GA-01 ballot (the 6-candidate field was Kingston/Farrell/Taylor/Montgomery/Yu/Penn). He filed for re-election to GA House HD-166 and won the 2026-05-19 R primary unopposed (100%, 10,673 votes); faces Pat Daley (D) 2026-11-03. Scored at state tier from official legis.ga.gov roll calls.

jesse-petrea.notes_set: Record corrected 2026-06-10: never a GA-01 candidate — sitting GA HD-166 rep, renominated unopposed 2026-05-19 (Ballotpedia; Savannah Tribune). Marquee record: YEA HB 481 LIFE Act (2019, 'Pro-life district, pro-life candidate'), YEA SB 319 constitutional carry (2022), YEA SB 202 election law (2021), YEA SB 233 vouchers (2023 fail + 2024 pass), YEA SB 140 minors gender-medicine ban (2023), YEA HB 267 Riley Gaines Act (2025), YEA SB 36 GA RFRA (2025), lead sponsor HB 1105 Criminal Alien Track & Report Act (2024 — model for the federal Laken Riley Act), YEA SB 345 vaccine-passport prohibition (2022). Watch-items: YEA HB 428 (2025) codifying IVF right (160–0; strict-personhood groups objected); JBS Freedom Index lifetime 45 / 2025 20% (spending votes) vs Turning Point Action 88 (Taxes 100%, Border 100%). No cross-party deviations found on any marquee vote. Signed U.S. Term Limits pledge.

0.note: YEA on HB 481 LIFE Act (2019, incl. fetal-personhood provisions; House passage + final). Quote: 'Pro-life district, pro-life candidate. No one should be surprised about my yes vote.'

1.note: Heartbeat law is restriction, not abolition; 2024 Q&A said the current law should not change. No abolition advocacy.

2.note: YEA on HB 428 (2025) codifying a right to IVF as practiced (passed 160–0); strict-personhood groups opposed codification.

YEA SB 140 (2023, ban on gender-transition hormones/surgery for minors); YEA HB 267 Riley Gaines Act (2025, sex-based sports/facility definitions); Y on SB 185 (2025, no state funds for inmate gender care).

0.note: YEA SB 233 Promise Scholarship vouchers both times (2023 failed vote + 2024 passage); Q&A: supports school choice and charter schools.

1.note: YEA SB 140 (2023) — bans gender-medicalization of minors (stronger than a consent requirement).
YEA SB 36, the Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (2025, House Vote #401) — state conscience protections.

2.note: Conflicting ratings: TPAction Taxes & Spending 100% and lead-sponsored 2025 tax-relief act, but JBS Freedom Index 2025 20% over spending votes. No clear position scored.

1.note: YEA SB 202 (2021) — photo/state-ID requirement for absentee ballots.

3.note: YEA SB 202 — restricted drop-box number/placement and third-party ballot handling.

4.note: YEA SB 202 — banned private funding of county election administration ('Zuckerbucks').

1.note: HB 1105 targets reporting of criminal aliens; Q&A decries illegal immigration depressing wages — but no documented position on deporting ALL illegal aliens incl. minors.

2.note: Lead sponsor, HB 1105 (2024) — penalties for sheriffs refusing ICE cooperation; model for the federal Laken Riley Act; co-sponsor HB 319 (2025); TPAction Border Security 100%.

0.note: YEA SB 319, Georgia Constitutional Carry Act (2022, House Vote #817).

1.note: 2024 Q&A on new gun laws: 'I do not support placing unconstitutional restrictions on the constitutional rights of law abiding citizens.'

set.office: State Representative (GA House District 176 · Waycross · House Majority Whip · sitting · 2026 re-election)

profile.education: B.S., Georgia Southern University; J.D., Florida Coastal School of Law
profile.background: Attorney (partner, Burchett & Kemp LLC); GA House since March 2019 special election; House Majority Caucus Whip; carried SB 68 tort reform (2025); Appropriations, Judiciary Non-Civil, Rules (ex-officio) committees
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — RECORD CORRECTED: prior record mis-filed him as a 2026 GA-01 federal candidate; he publicly DECLINED to run for GA-01 (June 2025, The Downballot) and was never on that ballot. He filed for re-election to GA House HD-176 and won the 2026-05-19 R primary 82.4%–17.6% over Michael Dockery; faces Marcus Ryan (D) 2026-11-03. Scored at state tier from official legis.ga.gov roll calls.

james-burchett.notes_set: Record corrected 2026-06-10: never a GA-01 candidate — said in June 2025 he would not run (The Downballot); sitting GA HD-176 rep and House Majority Whip, renominated 82.4% on 2026-05-19 (Ballotpedia/GA SoS). Sworn in 2019-03-18 (special-election runoff win 59.3%). Marquee record: YEA HB 481 LIFE Act final passage (Vote #343, 2019-03-29 — sworn in after the original House vote), YEA SB 319 constitutional carry (2022), YEA SB 202 election law (2021), YEA SB 233 vouchers (2023 fail + 2024 pass), YEA SB 140 minors gender-medicine ban (2023), YEA HB 267 Riley Gaines Act (2025), YEA SB 36 GA RFRA (2025), YEA HB 1105 immigration enforcement (2024, both votes), YEA SB 345 vaccine-passport prohibition (2022), carried SB 68 tort reform (2025, 91–82). Ratings: TPAction 93.5 (2026 session) / 100 lifetime key-issues vs JBS Freedom Index cumulative 48%. Signed U.S. Term Limits pledge. No party-break votes found on the marquee set.

0.note: YEA on HB 481 LIFE Act final passage (Agree Sen Sub, Vote #343, 2019-03-29, 92–78; incl. fetal-personhood provisions). Sworn in 2019-03-18, after the original House vote.

2.note: YEA SB 140 (2023, ban on gender-transition hormones/surgery for minors, Vote #243); YEA HB 267 Riley Gaines Act (2025, Vote #103).

0.note: YEA SB 233 Promise Scholarship vouchers both times — 2023 failed vote (#369) and 2024 passage (#697).

1.note: YEA SB 140 (2023) — bans gender-medicalization of minors (stronger than a consent requirement).
YEA SB 36, the Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (2025, House Vote #401).

2.note: Conflicting ratings: TPAction 93.5/100-lifetime vs JBS Freedom Index cumulative 48%. No clear deficit/BBA position scored.

1.note: YEA SB 202 (2021, Vote #296) — photo/state-ID requirement for absentee ballots.

3.note: YEA SB 202 — restricted drop boxes and third-party ballot handling.

4.note: YEA SB 202 — banned private funding of county election administration.

2.note: YEA HB 1105 Criminal Alien Track and Report Act (2024) on passage (#613) and final (#871) — penalties for non-cooperation with ICE (anti-sanctuary enforcement).

0.note: YEA SB 319, Georgia Constitutional Carry Act (2022, Vote #817); voted NAY on the motion to table.

profile.religion: Baptist (Mount Zion Baptist Church, Albany GA)
profile.education: B.A. political science, Morehouse College (1968); J.D., Emory University School of Law (1971)
profile.background: U.S. Army 1969–71; attorney in Columbus GA; U.S. House since 1993 (dean of GA delegation); Blue Dog Coalition + Congressional Black Caucus; Ranking Member, Appropriations Subcmte on Agriculture–Rural Development–FDA
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from Clerk roll calls + scorecards. Renominated unopposed in the 2026-05-19 D primary; faces Matt Day (R) in the general (Cook: Solid D).

sanford-bishop.notes_set: 17-term Blue Dog Democrat, GA-02 (SW Georgia). Current record is near-party-line liberal: Heritage Action 0% session / 9% lifetime; FRC Action 6% (118th); Reproductive Freedom for All 2024 score 100. Marquee: YEA WHPA abortion codification 2021+2022, NAY Born-Alive (2023), YEA Equality Act (2021, cosponsor), YEA Respect for Marriage Act (2022 both votes), NAY women's-sports bills (2023, 2025), NAY Parents Bill of Rights (2023), YEA H.R.1 For the People (2021), NO/NAY SAVE Act (2024, 2025), NAY H.R.2 Secure the Border (2023), YEA 2022 Assault Weapons Ban (own press release), NO on repealing the CMS health-worker vaccine mandate (2023). DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: YEA final Laken Riley Act (S.5, Roll 23, 2025-01-22, after Nay on House versions), YEA Tlaib censure (2023), YEA HALT Fentanyl (2023+2025), YEA Israel/Ukraine/Indo-Pacific aid + TikTok divestiture (2024), consistent NDAA YEA, YEA Fiscal Responsibility Act (2023). Historical (superseded): YEA partial-birth-abortion ban twice in 2003; NRA A-rating through the 2000s–2010s incl. Aye on Concealed Carry Reciprocity (2017) — NRA grade fell to C by 2020; YEA DOMA (1996). Pigford settlement fraud controversy (2011) noted in press.

0.note: On Dobbs: abortion 'choices should ultimately rest with a woman, her God and her doctor'; YEA on WHPA abortion codification — documented denial of legal personhood from conception.

1.note: YEA WHPA 2021 + 2022; NAY Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (2023); RFFA 2024 score 100. (Historical: YEA partial-birth ban ×2 in 2003 — superseded by current record.)

0.note: YEA Respect for Marriage Act (both 2022 votes), codifying recognition of same-sex marriage. (YEA DOMA 1996 superseded.)

1.note: Voted to codify same-sex-marriage recognition in federal law (RFMA final, Roll 513, 2022).

2.note: NAY on the Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act twice (2023 H.R.734; 2025 H.R.28) — declined to affirm sex-based categories.

4.note: YEA + cosponsor, Equality Act (2021) — adds SOGI as protected classes across federal law.

1.note: NAY on the Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5, 2023) — the House parental-notification/transparency bill.
YEA Equality Act, which expressly bars RFRA conscience defenses; also NAY on H.Con.Res.3 condemning attacks on pro-life facilities and churches (2023).

2.note: Heritage Action 0% (session) / 9% lifetime; FRC Action 6% — consistent votes against conservative fiscal positions.

1.note: NO on the SAVE Act (proof of citizenship for voter registration) in both 2024 and 2025.

2.note: YEA H.R.1 For the People Act (2021) — mandates expanded early voting nationwide, contrary to single-day in-person voting.

3.note: YEA H.R.1 — mandates no-excuse mail voting and drop boxes nationwide.

0.note: NAY on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (2023), whose core title funds completion of the border wall (party-line marquee vote).

1.note: NAY on H.R.30 Preventing Violence Against Women by Illegal Aliens Act (2025, expanded deportability). DEVIATION NOTED: YEA on final Laken Riley Act (S.5, Roll 23, 2025) — mandatory detention, after Nay on both House versions.

3.note: NAY on H.R.2, whose other core title is nationwide mandatory E-Verify.

0.note: Aye on Concealed Carry Reciprocity (2017) — permit reciprocity, not constitutional carry; no position on permitless carry.

1.note: YEA Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 (own press release confirms), YEA H.R.8 background checks (2021), YEA BSCA (2022, funds state red-flag programs). NRA grade fell A→C by 2020.

0.note: NO on repealing the CMS health-worker COVID vaccine mandate (H.R.497, 2023); NAY on ending the COVID emergency (H.R.382) and the foreign-traveler vaccine requirement (H.R.185).

profile.background: Former Trump White House Political Director (2019–21), longtime Trump aide; previously RNC and AIPAC staff; elected 2024 (runoff 62.6%, general 66.3%); Rules + Oversight committees; Chair, Small Business Subcmte on Innovation; co-chair, House Republican Israel Caucus
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from 119th-Congress Clerk roll calls + cosponsorships. Renominated unopposed 2026-05-19; general rematch vs Maura Keller (D) in safe-R GA-03.

brian-jack.notes_set: Freshman R, GA-03; Heritage Action 100%, FRC Action 100%, Club for Growth-endorsed. Marquee 119th record: YEA Born-Alive (2025), YEA OBBB (incl. Planned Parenthood Medicaid defund + NFA suppressor/SBR tax zero-out), YEA women's-sports act, YEA + cosponsor SAVE Act, YEA + cosponsor Laken Riley Act, YEA + cosponsor Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act, YEA Balanced Budget Amendment (2026), YEA DC anti-sanctuary act; cosponsor: Legal Workforce Act (E-Verify), Birthright Citizenship Act, Chloe Cole Act, Hearing Protection Act, H.R.38 carry reciprocity, National Right-to-Work, Dismantle DEI Act. FLAGS (foreign-policy lens): voted NAY on EVERY war-powers withdrawal resolution (Iran/Venezuela/Lebanon, 2025–26, incl. H.Con.Res.86 which passed with R defectors); former AIPAC employee, Israel Caucus co-chair, ~$163.8K pro-Israel 2024 donations (FEC-derived) — scored FALSE on the foreign-lobby-money and war-powers cells. NAY on Ukraine Support Act (2026). CR-pledge tension: Club for Growth said he opposes CRs, but YEA on the Sept 2025 CR and the Nov 2025 shutdown-ending CR. No leftward cross-party deviations found.

0.note: Consistent pro-life voting record: YEA Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Roll 27, 2025); YEA OBBB incl. Planned Parenthood Medicaid defund; FRC Action 100%.

1.note: No abolition (vs. restriction) advocacy documented; no Life at Conception Act cosponsorship among his 111 cosponsored bills.

3.note: YEA Born-Alive Act — mandates care for infants surviving abortion, rejecting quality-of-life rationing of newborns.

2.note: YEA Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Roll 12, 2025); cosponsor of the Chloe Cole Act (detransitioner right of action) in both 2025 and 2026 versions.

4.note: Cosponsor, Dismantle DEI Act of 2025; YEA on removing gender-identity categories from women's sports.

1.note: Cosponsor, Chloe Cole Act (2025 + 2026) — accountability for gender-transition interventions on minors.

0.note: YEA + cosponsor, Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (H.R.1919, Roll 201, 2025).

2.note: YEA on the Balanced Budget Amendment (H.J.Res.139, Roll 95, 2026); Heritage Action 100%.

4.note: Cosponsor of the Fair Access to Banking Act (anti-debanking) — directionally anti-ESG-capture but no direct ESG/antitrust position documented.

1.note: YEA + cosponsor, SAVE Act (proof of citizenship for registration); YEA barring noncitizen voting in D.C. elections (H.R.884).
YEA Laken Riley Act ×2 (mandatory DETENTION of criminal aliens) and NAY on Haiti TPS mandate — strong enforcement record, but no documented position on deporting ALL illegal aliens incl. minors.

2.note: YEA, District of Columbia Federal Immigration Compliance Act (anti-sanctuary, Roll 171, 2025).

3.note: Cosponsor, Legal Workforce Act (H.R.251) — nationwide mandatory E-Verify.

4.note: Cosponsor, Birthright Citizenship Act (H.R.569); cosponsor, Nuclear Family Priority Act (ends chain-migration categories).

0.note: Cosponsor, Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2025 (interstate recognition for permitless-carry-state residents).

1.note: Cosponsor: Protecting Privacy in Purchases Act (bans gun-store merchant codes) and Gun Owner Registration Information Protection Act (anti-registry).

2.note: Cosponsor, Hearing Protection Act (removes suppressors from the NFA); voted for OBBB, which zeroed the $200 NFA tax on suppressors/SBRs — partial NFA rollback.

0.note: Voted NAY on every Article I war-powers withdrawal resolution 2025–26 (Iran ×4 incl. H.Con.Res.86 which PASSED, Venezuela ×2, Lebanon, hemisphere strikes) — against requiring congressional authorization.

1.note: Opposed every resolution directing withdrawal of U.S. forces from ongoing hostilities (2025–26 series).

3.note: Former AIPAC employee; co-chairs the House Republican Israel Caucus; FEC-derived reporting shows ~$163,814 in pro-Israel donations to his 2024 campaign incl. AIPAC PAC.
